# Update CI pipelines and build scripts for new archive substrate

## Context
This is bucket B3 of Epic #11187 (Phase 4).
The release and data-sync pipelines need to be updated to target the new single-root `archive/` substrate instead of `issue-archive/`, `pr-archive/`, etc.

## The Problem
Pipelines currently hardcode paths like `issue-archive/`. This will break once the new structure is in place.

## The Architectural Reality
- `.github/workflows/data-sync-pipeline.yml`
- `buildScripts/release/publish.mjs`

## The Fix
Update the push paths in `data-sync-pipeline.yml` and the archive-cutting logic in `publish.mjs` to map to the new `archive/{type}/v*/` structure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `data-sync-pipeline.yml` uses the new archive paths.
- [ ] `publish.mjs` archive-cutting logic aligns with the lazy 100-item ordinal chunking.

## Out of Scope
- General CI refactoring outside of the archive path changes.

## Related
- Epic #11187

Origin Session ID: 2c4aa4df-2628-45ae-a9c2-156fd9308f21

- 2026-05-13T10:18:55Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e223b9d` - "feat(github-workflow): pipeline + publish archive substrate updates for Epic #11187 (#11286) (#11296)

Implements B3 of Epic #11187 7-bucket fan-out — update CI pipelines + build scripts
to target the new single-root archive/ substrate while preserving legacy paths
during the migration transition.

**data-sync-pipeline.yml** (AC1):
- Expanded pages-repo sync to cover pulls/, discussions/, archive/ in addition to
  existing issues/ + issue-archive/. Legacy paths (issue-archive/, pr-archive/)
  retained during transition; will drop in follow-up PR post-corpus-migration.
- Refactored as loop with missing-dir tolerance: each path's rm/cp guarded so
  transitional states (e.g., archive/ not yet populated, pr-archive/ removed
  post-AC8) don't break sync.

**publish.mjs Step 6** (AC2):
- Added documentation comment clarifying that runFullSync writes archived items to
  new lazy-ordinal-chunked shape via archivePath() helper. The existing
  `git add .` is intentionally broad to capture both new archive/ moves AND
  residual legacy-path moves during transition. No logic change — substrate
  already routes correctly via the syncer.

V-B-A: YAML syntax verified via js-yaml; diff scope bounded to 2 files (+21/-5).
No regression risk — pipeline changes are additive (new paths) + tolerant of
missing dirs during transition.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
